book_claims.claim: Regular consumption supports disease prevention and longevity
book_claims.mechanism: Part of fibre-rich food group providing vitamins, oligo-elements and phytochemicals processed by gut microbiome into protective metabolites

book_claims.claim: Five servings per week associated with 40-60% reduction in heart attack risk
book_claims.mechanism: Provide polyunsaturated omega-3 fatty acids (walnuts) and omega-6 fatty acids plus plant sterols (almonds, hazelnuts, pine nuts) that reduce LDL-cholesterol

book_claims.claim: Part of cholesterol-lowering food portfolio that lowers LDL-cholesterol by 13%
book_claims.mechanism: Combined effect of nuts with soy protein, oats, barley and psyllium on cholesterol absorption and synthesis

book_claims.claim: Indo-Mediterranean diet with walnuts and almonds reduced non-fatal heart attacks by 50% and sudden cardiac death by 60%
book_claims.mechanism: Omega-3 and omega-6 fatty acids reduce inflammation and improve lipid profile

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis (Aune et al., BMC Medicine 2016) of 20 prospective studies found 28g/day of nuts associated with 22% lower all-cause mortality, 21% lower CVD risk, and 15% lower cancer risk in a dose-response relationship.

PREDIMED trial analysis confirmed that participants randomized to Mediterranean diet supplemented with 30g mixed nuts/day had 28% lower CVD events, 46% lower peripheral artery disease, and improved cognitive function versus control (Estruch et al., 2018).

Nurses' Health Study and Health Professionals Follow-Up Study found nut consumption 5+ times/week associated with significant reduction in total mortality and cause-specific mortality for CVD, cancer, and respiratory disease (Bao et al., NEJM 2013).

culinary_pairings.source: Traditional dessert pairing; fat + fibre moderate glycemic response
Complementary unsaturated fatty acid profiles; PREDIMED tested both
Combined polyphenol profiles for additive cardiovascular benefit
Cholesterol-lowering food portfolio combining nuts, oats, and barley
Traditional sweetener with nuts; baklava, nougat, nut bars

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Walnuts: rich in ALA omega-3. Almonds/hazelnuts: rich in oleic acid (MUFA). Pine nuts: rich in pinolenic acid. All reduce LDL without lowering HDL.
Compete with cholesterol for intestinal absorption, reducing circulating LDL-cholesterol. Pistachios and almonds are richest nut sources.
Almonds: alpha-tocopherol. Walnuts: gamma-tocopherol + ellagitannins. Hazelnuts: proanthocyanidins. Diverse antioxidant profiles across nut types.

synergies.description: Both provide complementary unsaturated fatty acid profiles; PREDIMED tested both as Mediterranean diet supplements
Walnuts provide plant-based omega-3 (ALA) complementing marine omega-3 (EPA/DHA) from fish
